description_long: This is a twin-engine, high-wing utility airplane with a boxy fuselage and a conventional tail. The aircraft features two turboprop engines mounted on the wings, each with a three-bladed propeller. The landing gear is fixed and tricycle-type, with visible main gear under the wings and a nose wheel. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and is enclosed, with multiple windows providing visibility. The wings are straight and extend laterally from the top of the fuselage, and the tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The exterior is painted in a camouflage pattern with tan, black, and gray tones, and there are visible access ladders and hatches on the fuselage.
description: A twin-engine, high-wing utility airplane with a boxy fuselage, fixed tricycle landing gear, and a camouflage paint scheme. It features two turboprop engines, a conventional tail, and an enclosed forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin turboprop engines, high-mounted straight wings, and the nose with cockpit windows.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the boxy fuselage, access ladder, and the fixed main and nose landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section with a single vertical stabilizer, horizontal stabilizers, and the rear fuselage.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the opposite side of the fuselage, wing, and access features, mirroring the left side.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
